title: Unit 12: Building the Simple Stem - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 12 introduces the methodology for constructing the Simple, or Perfective, Stem in Modern Greek. This is the absolute foundation for all non-continuous tenses, including the Simple Future and the Subjunctive. Athina, what is the 'S-Group' rule for these stems?
Αν το ρήμα τελειώνει σε -νω, -θω ή -ζω, το θέμα αλλάζει σε σίγμα. Διαβάζω – διαβάσω. Κλείνω – κλείσω.
Precisely. The 'Sigma' stem is the most prevalent. Next, we have the 'X-Group', which applies to verbs ending in velar consonants—the 'throat' sounds. Athina, what are the transformations for 'X'?
Τα ρήματα σε -κω, -γω, -χω ή -σκω αλλάζουν σε ξι. Ανοίγω – ανοίξω. Τρέχω – τρέξω.
It is noteworthy that certain extremely common '-zo' verbs—such as 'paizo'—are irregular and belong to the 'X-Group'. Athina, demonstrate the 'Psi' or 'PS-Group' for labial 'lip' sounds.
Τα ρήματα σε -πω, -βω, -φω ή -εύω αλλάζουν σε ψι. Γράφω – γράψω. Δουλεύω – δουλέψω.
Lastly, we must address the monosyllabic irregular stems. These verbs are the most high-frequency in the Greek language. Athina, please state the stems for 'Vlepo' and 'Leo'.
Βλέπω – δω. Λέω – πω. Πίνω – πιω. Βρίσκω – βρω. Πηγαίνω – πάω.

Note that because these stems are so brief, the lexical stress must shift to the ending. This concludes our academic overview of Unit 12. Mastering these stem transformations is a core requirement for grammatical dexterity in Modern Greek.
